responses: Hello! I am the University Assistant. Ask me about admissions, courses, fees, hostel, exams, and more.
Hi! Welcome to the university help desk. How can I assist you today?
Greetings! I can help with admissions, scholarships, departments, library, and campus facilities.
Goodbye! Visit the student portal or email contact@university.edu if you need more help.
See you later! Best of luck with your studies.
Take care! Feel free to ask anytime about university services.
You are welcome! Is there anything else about the university I can help with?
Glad I could help! Ask me about admissions, courses, or campus life anytime.
My pleasure! Good luck with your academic journey.

I am an ML-based university chatbot. I answer questions about admissions, fees, courses, hostel, library, exams, scholarships, departments, and campus services using intent classification (TF-IDF + Logistic Regression).
You can ask me about: admission process, fee structure, scholarships, programs, hostel, library timings, exam schedule, results, contact details, and more.
I use Natural Language Processing to match your message to trained intents and reply with pre-defined university information. Type your question in plain English!
Apply online via the university admission portal. Submit your CNIC/ID, academic transcripts, passport-size photo, and the completed application form before the deadline.
Steps: (1) Check program eligibility on the website, (2) Fill the online form, (3) Pay the application fee, (4) Upload documents, (5) Track status on the portal.

Admission is open each semester. Required documents typically include matric/intermediate or bachelor marksheets, ID copy, and recent photographs. Contact admissions@university.edu for program-specific rules.

Fees vary by program (e.g., Engineering, Business, CS). Undergraduate programs are typically billed per semester. See the official fee chart on the university website under Admissions → Fee Structure.
Besides tuition, there may be admission fee, lab charges, and optional hostel/mess fees. Scholarships can reduce tuition for eligible students.
For exact amounts, check the latest fee notification on the portal or email finance@university.edu with your program name.
General: contact@university.edu | Phone: +92-42-1234567 (Mon–Fri, 9 AM–5 PM). Admissions: admissions@university.edu.
Visit https://university.edu/contact for department-wise emails and campus map.
Student support: support@university.edu. For emergencies on campus, contact the security desk listed on the website.
We offer merit-based, need-based, and special-category scholarships. Apply through the scholarship section on the student portal with income certificate and academic records.
Merit scholarships often require minimum GPA (e.g., 3.5+). Need-based aid requires financial documentation. Deadlines are announced each semester on the notice board.
Contact the Financial Aid Office at scholarships@university.edu for eligibility and required forms.
Undergraduate: BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Electrical Engineering, BBA, and more. Postgraduate: MS CS, MBA, MPhil programs in select departments.
Visit Academics → Programs on the website for full list, credit hours, and duration of each degree.
Each program has its own entry test and eligibility (e.g., FSc Pre-Engineering for engineering). Check the prospectus for details.
Main departments: Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Business Administration, Mathematics, and Physics.
Department offices are in their respective blocks. Timings: usually 9 AM–4 PM on working days.
For HOD contact and faculty list, see the department page on the university website.
Separate hostels for male and female students are available on campus. Apply through the hostel office after admission confirmation.
Facilities typically include furnished rooms, mess, laundry, study areas, and Wi-Fi. Hostel fee is charged per semester in addition to tuition.
Contact hostel@university.edu for vacancy, rules, and the hostel application form.

patterns: how many books can i issue
responses: Central library is open Monday–Saturday, 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM. Bring your student ID to borrow books.
Students can usually issue 2–3 books for 14 days. Renewals are allowed if no reservation exists.
Online catalog and digital resources are available through the library portal on the student dashboard.
Exam dates are published on the student portal and notice boards 2–3 weeks before exams.
Midterms are usually in week 8–9; finals at the end of the semester. Check your course outline for exact dates.
Download the official date sheet from Academics → Examination on the portal.
Results are published on the student portal under Results → Semester. Login with your student ID.
Result declaration is announced via email and SMS. Typically 2–4 weeks after final exams.
For grade disputes, contact the examination office within the appeal window mentioned on the notice.
Campus offers cricket, football, badminton, table tennis, and a gym. Register with the sports society at the start of semester.
Annual sports week is held each spring. Trials are announced on the notice board and social media.
Sports facilities are free for enrolled students during posted hours.
The main campus is at University Road, City Name. Google Maps: search 'University Name Campus'.
Public transport routes 12 and 45 stop near the main gate. Visitor parking is available near the admin block.
Download the campus map from the website under About → Campus.
Access the student portal at https://portal.university.edu with your student ID and password issued at enrollment.
Use the portal for course registration, fee vouchers, results, and notices. Reset password via 'Forgot Password' with your registered email.
For LMS/course materials, open the Learning tab after login. Contact IT helpdesk@university.edu for technical issues.
Most courses require at least 75% attendance to sit in final exams. Medical leave may be accepted with documentation.
Attendance is tracked in the LMS or manual registers. Check with your course instructor for lab/practical sessions.
Submit leave applications to the department office for approved absences.
The Career Development Center helps with internships, CV reviews, and on-campus recruitment drives.
Register on the career portal for internship listings. Final-year students get priority for industry partnerships.
Email careers@university.edu for appointment with a career counselor.
Cafeteria and food courts are in the student center block, open 8 AM – 6 PM on weekdays.
University shuttle buses run on fixed routes from major city points. Route map is on the transport office notice board.
Transport pass can be purchased at the admin office each semester.
Campus Wi-Fi is available for students. Connect using credentials from IT after enrollment.
Computer labs are in the CS/Engineering blocks. Printing is available at the library and lab helpdesk.
IT Support: ithelp@university.edu or visit the IT desk in the admin building.
Apply for transcripts and certificates at the Examination/Registrar office with ID and fee challan.
Processing usually takes 7–14 working days. Degree convocation dates are announced after completion of requirements.
For employer verification, use the online degree verification link on the university website.
This chatbot is a university Machine Learning project. It classifies user messages into intents using TF-IDF vectorization and Logistic Regression, then returns matching responses from intents.json.
Pipeline: text preprocessing (lowercase, remove punctuation/stopwords) → TF-IDF features → classifier → random response from the matched intent. Fallback messages are used when confidence is low.
To improve the bot, add more patterns and responses in intents.json and re-run the application to retrain the model.
This university assistant chatbot was developed as a Machine Learning course project.
For project demo details, refer to the README and source code in the project repository.
The system demonstrates intent-based dialogue using Python, scikit-learn, and NLTK.
